en: After an earlier world had been destroyed, the gods decided that people must live again. Quetzalcoatl, the Feathered Serpent, took on the task of finding the bones of the former race. He went down to Mictlan, the land of the dead, and stood before its lord Mictlantecuhtli. He asked for the bones so that new human beings could be made.

Mictlantecuhtli said Quetzalcoatl could have the bones if he walked around the underworld while sounding a shell trumpet. The shell had no holes, so Quetzalcoatl called on worms to pierce it and bees to make it sing. The ruler then allowed him to take the bones, but he secretly ordered a trap to be set in his path. Quetzalcoatl seized the bones and ran for the upper world.

As he fled, the ground opened and he fell into a pit. The bones dropped from his arms and broke into pieces of many sizes. Quetzalcoatl gathered them up and carried them to the place where the gods were waiting. He gave his own blood over the bones, and from them living people rose into the new world.
zh: 在更早的一个世界毁灭之后，众神决定要让人类再次出现。羽蛇神魁札尔科亚特尔承担了寻找旧人类骸骨的任务。他下到冥界米克特兰，站在冥界之主米克特兰特库特里面前。他请求取走那些骨头，好让新的世人被造出来。
米克特兰特库特里说，只要魁札尔科亚特尔能一边绕行冥界一边吹响海螺号角，就可以带走骨头。那只海螺本来没有孔洞，于是魁札尔科亚特尔叫来虫子钻孔，又让蜜蜂在里面发声。冥界之主表面上答应了他，却暗中命人沿路设下陷阱。魁札尔科亚特尔抱起骨头，向上界飞快逃去。
他逃走时，大地忽然裂开，他掉进了一个深坑。骨头从他怀里散落出来，摔成了大小不一的碎片。魁札尔科亚特尔把碎骨重新收起，带回众神等待的地方。他用自己的血洒在骨头上，于是活着的人类就在新的世界里诞生了。

en: When Vasilisa's mother was dying, she gave her daughter a small doll and told her to keep it always. After the mother died, Vasilisa's father married again. Her stepmother and stepsisters were harsh to her and loaded her with work. One night they put out every fire in the house and sent her into the forest to ask Baba Yaga for flame.

Deep in the woods, Vasilisa found Baba Yaga's hut standing on chicken legs in a clearing. She spoke to the hut, and it turned its door toward her. Baba Yaga arrived in her mortar, steering with a pestle and sweeping away her tracks with a broom. The old witch said Vasilisa could have fire only if she finished the housework and sorted the heaps of grain.

Vasilisa fed the little doll, and the doll helped her complete every task before morning. Baba Yaga saw that the work was done and asked how the girl had managed it. When Vasilisa said that her mother's blessing helped her, Baba Yaga shoved her away and gave her a skull lantern with burning eyes. Vasilisa carried it home, and its fire burned her stepmother and stepsisters to ashes, leaving her alive and free.
zh: 瓦西丽莎的母亲临终前，给了女儿一个小布偶，并叮嘱她一直带在身边。母亲死后，瓦西丽莎的父亲又娶了新妻子。继母和两个继姐对她十分刻薄，把重活都压在她身上。一天夜里，她们故意弄灭了家里的火，逼她到森林里向芭芭雅嘎去讨火种。
瓦西丽莎走进林中深处，看见空地上有一间长着鸡脚的小屋。她对小屋说了话，小屋便转过门来对着她。芭芭雅嘎坐着石臼飞来，用杵掌舵，再用扫帚抹去自己的踪迹。老巫婆说，只有瓦西丽莎替她做完家务并把成堆的谷物分拣好，才能带走火。
瓦西丽莎喂了小布偶，小布偶便在夜里帮她完成了一切活计。芭芭雅嘎看见工作都做完了，就问她是靠什么做到的。瓦西丽莎回答说，是母亲的祝福在帮助她，于是芭芭雅嘎立刻把她赶走，并给了她一盏长着燃烧眼睛的骷髅灯。瓦西丽莎提着骷髅灯回到家，灯里的火焰把继母和两个继姐烧成了灰，她自己则活了下来并得到自由。

en: Odysseus landed on an island with his men and found a cave full of food and pens for sheep and goats. He chose to wait inside to meet the owner instead of taking the supplies and leaving at once. The cave belonged to Polyphemus, a Cyclops who rolled a huge stone across the entrance when he returned. Odysseus and his men were trapped inside with the giant.

Polyphemus ignored their pleas and began seizing the men to eat them. Odysseus knew that killing the Cyclops outright would leave them sealed in the cave behind the great stone. He offered the giant strong wine and told him that his name was Nobody. When Polyphemus fell into a heavy sleep, Odysseus and his men drove a sharpened stake into his single eye and blinded him.

The Cyclops shouted for help, but when the others asked who was hurting him, he answered that Nobody was attacking him. At dawn Polyphemus opened the cave and felt the backs of his sheep as he let them out, hoping to catch the Greeks. Odysseus tied his men beneath the animals and clung under the largest ram, so they slipped outside unseen. Once he had reached the ship, he revealed his true name, and Polyphemus called on his father Poseidon to punish him on the long voyage home.
zh: 奥德修斯带着部下登上一座岛屿，发现了一处洞穴，里面堆着食物，还圈着羊和山羊。别人想拿了东西就走，奥德修斯却决定留下来，等洞穴的主人回来相见。那洞穴属于独眼巨人波吕斐摩斯，他回到洞里后用一块巨石堵住了洞口。奥德修斯和同伴就这样被困在了巨人里面。
波吕斐摩斯根本不理会他们的求饶，抓起奥德修斯的部下就生吃。奥德修斯知道如果直接杀死巨人，大家也无法搬开那块堵门的大石头。他先拿出烈酒给巨人喝，又骗他说自己的名字叫“无人”。等波吕斐摩斯醉倒熟睡后，奥德修斯和同伴把削尖的木桩刺进了他唯一的眼睛。
独眼巨人痛得大喊求救，别的独眼巨人赶来询问时，他却说是“无人”在害他。天亮后，波吕斐摩斯挪开石头放羊出去，一边摸着羊背，一边想把希腊人留在洞里。奥德修斯把同伴绑在羊腹下，自己也伏在最壮的一只公羊下面，于是众人悄悄逃了出去。等他回到船上后，他向巨人报出了自己的真名，波吕斐摩斯便向父亲波塞冬祈求，让奥德修斯在归途中受尽磨难。

en: When the army called for men to serve, Mulan saw that her aged father's name was on the draft rolls. He was too old and weak to endure another campaign, and her younger brother was still a child. Mulan decided that her family could not send him to war in that condition. She bought a horse and equipment and left home disguised as a man in her father's place.

Mulan joined the army and traveled with the troops over long distances through cold camps and dangerous marches. She kept her identity hidden while training, riding, and fighting beside the other soldiers. Year after year she served in the field and faced the same hardships as the men around her. Her skill and courage helped her survive until the war was won.

After victory, the ruler offered rewards and office to the returning soldiers. Mulan asked only for a swift horse so that she could go back to her home. When she put on her old clothes again and stepped out before her former comrades, they were astonished to see that she was a woman. They had fought beside her for years without knowing who she truly was.
zh: 朝廷征兵的时候，木兰看到军帖上写着自己父亲的名字。父亲年老体衰，已经难以再经受战场的劳苦，而弟弟又还年幼，无法代替他出征。木兰知道家里不能让父亲硬撑着上路。于是她买来马匹和装备，女扮男装代父从军。
木兰进入军中后，跟随大军长途跋涉，穿过荒野，也住过寒冷的营地。她始终把自己的身份隐藏起来，像其他士兵一样操练、骑马和作战。许多年里，她一直在军中服役，经历了和身边男子一样的艰苦。靠着本领和胆气，她一直活到战争结束。
凯旋之后，君主赏赐归来的将士，还想授给木兰官职。木兰没有接受这些封赏，只请求一匹快马，好让自己尽快回家。回到家中后，她换上旧时的衣裳，再次出现在旧日同袍面前。那些和她并肩作战多年的士兵这才惊讶地发现，她原来是个女子。

en: Aladdin was a poor young man when a sorcerer came to him pretending to be a relative. The sorcerer led him to a hidden cave and ordered him to bring back an old lamp from deep inside. Aladdin entered the cavern and found treasures there, but he refused to hand over the lamp before he was pulled out. The sorcerer sealed the cave in anger and left him trapped below the earth.

While searching for a way out, Aladdin used a magic ring and summoned a spirit that carried him home. Later he rubbed the dusty lamp and discovered that it held a far more powerful genie bound to serve its master. With the genie's help, Aladdin gained wealth, fine clothes, and a splendid palace. He used this new fortune to win the hand of the princess.

The sorcerer returned and tricked the princess into giving away the old lamp, then used the genie to carry the palace away with her inside. Aladdin was left with nothing but the magic ring and set out to find her. He reached the stolen palace, outwitted the sorcerer, and recovered the lamp. Once the lamp was back in his hands, the genie restored his power, and Aladdin destroyed his enemy and brought the princess home safely.
zh: 阿拉丁原本是个贫穷的年轻人，后来有个术士冒充亲戚来接近他。术士把他带到一处隐秘的地穴前，命他下去取回一盏旧灯。阿拉丁进入地下洞窟后看见了许多宝物，但他坚持要先被拉出去再交出神灯。术士恼怒之下封住洞口，把他独自困在了地下。
阿拉丁在绝境中摸索时，无意间用到了魔戒，召出了一个精灵，把他送回了家。后来他擦拭那盏旧灯，才发现灯中住着一个更强大的神灵，会服从灯主的命令。靠着神灯的力量，阿拉丁得到了财富、华服和宏伟的宫殿。凭借这一切，他终于娶到了公主。
术士后来又回来，骗公主用旧灯换新灯，把神灯重新骗到了自己手里，又命令灯神把整座宫殿连同公主一起搬走。阿拉丁转眼失去了一切，只能依靠魔戒去寻找公主的下落。他找到那座被夺走的宫殿后设法骗过术士，重新夺回了神灯。神灯回到手中后，阿拉丁再次命令灯神，除掉了敌人，并把公主平安带了回来。

en: Moses led the Israelites out of Egypt after Pharaoh finally allowed them to leave. Families set out together with their animals and possessions, moving away from the land where they had lived in bondage. Before long Pharaoh changed his mind and sent chariots and soldiers after them. The fleeing people soon found themselves trapped between the sea and the advancing army.

Fear spread through the camp as the Egyptians drew near behind them. Moses stood before the waters and stretched out his hand at God's command. A strong wind drove back the sea and opened a path across the seabed. The Israelites entered the passage and crossed with walls of water on both sides.

When the last of the Israelites had reached the far shore, the Egyptian forces rushed into the path after them. Moses stretched out his hand again, and the waters returned to their place. The sea closed over the chariots, the horses, and the soldiers of Pharaoh. The Israelites saw their pursuers destroyed and continued their journey into the wilderness.
zh: 法老终于答应放人后，摩西带领以色列人离开了埃及。各家各户带着家人、牲畜和随身财物一起上路，离开那片长期受奴役的土地。可是不久之后，法老又改变了主意，派出战车和军兵追赶他们。逃亡的人群很快就被逼到海边，前方是海水，后方是追兵。
看见埃及军队逼近，营中顿时充满了恐惧。摩西遵照上帝的命令，站在海边向海伸出手。强风整夜吹起，把海水向两边推开，在海中露出一条道路。以色列人便从这条路上走过去，两边都是高高立起的海水。
等最后一个以色列人到达对岸后，埃及的战车和士兵也冲进了海中的道路。摩西再次伸手，海水便回到了原处。汹涌的海浪淹没了法老的战车、马匹和军兵。以色列人看见追赶他们的人已经灭没，便继续向旷野前行。
